QF.id: Issue Management - General Obligations and Due Diligence_67

question.text: When do partly-paid locked-in shares complete their lock-in period?

answer.text: 3 years from the date of allotment of the partly-paid shares
18 months from the date when the last call is made
3 years from the date when they become fully paid-up and rank pari-passu with the shares of the public
1 year from the date of listing

explanation.text: **The Logic:** For partly-paid locked-in shares, the lock-in period of 3 years ends only 3 years after the shares become fully paid-up and rank pari-passu with public shares. The lock-in clock effectively resets when they become fully paid. **The Trap:** 3 years from allotment date sounds logical but ignores the partly-paid nature — shares that are not fully paid up are not comparable to public shares and thus need the full lock-in from the pari-passu date. **Cross-Exam Context:** Partly-paid share lock-in is a specialized topic in NISM Series IX that tests deep understanding of lock-in mechanics. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Think of it this way — the lock-in protects public investors, so it only counts from the date when the promoter shares are on equal footing with public shares (fully paid and pari-passu).
